summary: Completed frontend-only validation of upgraded Sorting visualizer on public URL. Core controls, animations, pointer tags, stats, step tracker, route navigation, and sorted confirmation work. One functional gap found in pseudocode highlight accuracy.

ui_bugs.issue: Active pseudocode line can be semantically incorrect for current operation (e.g., compare step highlights recursive line in Quick Sort), reducing instructional accuracy.

integration_issues.issue: Step metadata line numbers in sortingAlgorithms do not consistently map to displayed pseudocode intent.

action_items: Align each emitted step.line with the exact pseudocode statement for that algorithm.
Add a guard test asserting active pseudocode text matches operation type (pivot/compare/swap/merge/etc.).
critical_code_review_comments: sortingAlgorithms.js uses hardcoded line values that are inconsistent with pseudocode arrays, causing misleading highlight states during execution.
Instructional UX regression risk: users can see correct animation but wrong algorithm line highlighted, undermining learning objective.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated /sorting end-to-end with public URL and data-testid selectors. Confirmed algorithm options, sliders, generate/start/pause/resume/reset, pointer tags, color states (compare/swap/pivot/sorted), live stats/steps, and sorted success message. Reproduce remaining bug by running Quick Sort at low speed and comparing active step title vs highlighted pseudocode line.
rca of the issue: RCA: Step metadata and pseudocode model are decoupled. Repro: set Quick Sort + speed 1 + Start; when title shows 'Compare index 0 with pivot', highlighted line can be '4. recursively sort right partition'. Root cause is algorithm step.line assignments that do not reliably correspond to pseudocode item order/meaning. Mitigation: centralize per-algorithm step-type-to-line mapping and validate with UI-level assertions.
